description_long: This fire hydrant features a robust cylindrical body painted in a faded yellow, topped with a domed cap that is weathered to a pale, almost white color with hints of pinkish rust. The hydrant has three prominent hose connection outlets, each capped and secured with a chain, with the chains showing signs of oxidation. The surface is textured and slightly rough, with visible casting marks and some areas of chipped paint, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. The central body is flanked by two side outlets and a larger front outlet, all with circular caps. The top of the hydrant is fitted with an operating nut, used for opening the water valve. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior, and the hydrant is shown isolated without any curb or sidewalk context.
description: A weathered yellow fire hydrant with a pale, rust-stained domed cap, three capped hose outlets, and attached chains.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet cap, two side outlets, and attached chains.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the right side outlet and the domed cap's weathering.
description_view_2: Rear view with the back of the hydrant body and less visible outlet details.
description_view_3: Left side view focusing on the left outlet and the hydrant's textured surface.
